lesson.title: Consumer Protection Act
lesson.explanation: The CPA protects consumers in SA. Key rights: 6-month implied warranty on new goods (defective = repair, replace, or refund), 5-day cooling-off period for direct marketing purchases, right to honest information (no misleading advertising), plain language contracts, right to cancel fixed-term contracts on 20 business days notice. Defective goods: return within 6 months, seller must prove it wasn't defective at time of sale. Complaints: National Consumer Commission (012 428 7000). The CPA does not apply to B2B transactions above threshold.

Describe the key regulation or standard that governs Consumer Protection Act in South Africa.

practice_problems.explanation: SA has specific standards for each trade.

misconceptions.misconception: You don't need formal training for Consumer Protection Act
misconceptions.correction: Proper training and certification are required for safety and legal compliance in SA.
misconceptions.why_students_think_this: People see informal work and assume no qualifications are needed.
